title: Router setup + 4-step install guide
subtitle: Pick the right hardware, follow 4 steps, and every device on your home network — TV, console, smart speaker — gets seamless VPN.

heroCta.secondary: I already have a compatible router

step1.title: Prepare hardware
step1.subtitle: These five models are field-tested with k2r and span entry-level to pro-grade budgets.

redmi-ax6s.name: Redmi AX6S (RB03)
redmi-ax6s.tagline: The OpenWrt community workhorse — best price-to-performance
pros: Largest OpenWrt community install base; firmware is mature
Cheap on second-hand markets — units in good condition for ~¥150
Dozens of step-by-step flashing guides online
cons: Only gigabit WAN/LAN — can't saturate 1Gbps uplinks
redmi-ax6s.fit: 300M-500M home broadband / users new to flashing

xiaomi-ax3000t.name: Xiaomi AX3000T (RA82)
xiaomi-ax3000t.tagline: The sweet spot — slightly stronger than AX6S
pros: Slightly faster CPU than AX6S; full 2402Mbps Wi-Fi
cons: Flashing is slightly trickier than AX6S

glinet-flint2.name: GL.iNet Flint 2 (GL-MT6000)
glinet-flint2.tagline: Ships with OpenWrt — zero flashing required
pros: OpenWrt out of the box — no flashing risk
Dual 2.5G WAN/LAN ports for gigabit-plus broadband
cons: Limited availability in mainland China retail channels
glinet-flint2.fit: Gigabit broadband / users who want no-flash convenience

j4125-router.name: J4125 4-port gigabit soft router
pros: Cheap on second-hand / OEM markets — from ~¥400
Run iStoreOS, OpenWrt, or iKuai — your choice
cons: No Wi-Fi — needs a separate access point
j4125-router.fit: Sidecar router / households that already have a Wi-Fi AP

n100-router.name: N100 4×2.5G soft router
n100-router.tagline: Performance monster — won't bottleneck for 5-10 years
pros: Virtualize OpenWrt + AdGuard + NAS on one box
i226 2.5G ports match modern fiber broadband
n100-router.fit: Gigabit-plus broadband / power users running multiple services

step2.title: Install the OS
step2.subtitle: The path depends on hardware type. Flashing carries brick risk — newcomers should consider a pre-flashed Overleap router (see bottom of page).
step2.warning: ⚠️ Flashing has bricking risk. Always back up the stock firmware first; if you're not comfortable, choose the pre-flashed Overleap router service below.

arm.title: Flash OpenWrt firmware
arm.intro: ARM routers need third-party OpenWrt firmware. Common choices are ImmortalWrt or mainline OpenWrt. Core steps:
steps: Find the ImmortalWrt or OpenWrt sysupgrade image for your model on the OpenWrt forum, GitHub Releases, or 恩山论坛 (Right.com)
Flash via SSH / TFTP / U-Boot (procedure varies by model)
Open 192.168.1.1 in a browser, set the root password and WAN config

x86.title: Install OS on soft router
x86.intro: x86 soft routers are stripped-down PCs — boot from USB to install. We recommend iStoreOS (OpenWrt-based, friendlier UI):
steps: Download the iStoreOS img (https://fw0.koolcenter.com/iStoreOS/) or vanilla OpenWrt x86_64 image
Write it to a USB stick with balenaEtcher
Insert USB into the soft router; in BIOS, set USB as boot device
From the temporary system, dd the image to the disk (/dev/sda)
Power off, remove USB, boot from disk into iStoreOS

step3.title: Install k2r
step3.subtitle: SSH into the router and run this one-liner — it auto-detects ARM64 / x86_64 / ARMv7 and downloads the matching binary.
step3.explanation: The script does three things: ① download the k2r binary for your CPU; ② install an /etc/init.d service; ③ start k2r immediately. After that, open http://<router-IP>:9000 in your browser to reach the admin panel.

items.a: OpenWrt usually ships wget-ssl. Use curl instead: curl -fsSL https://overleap.io/i/k2r | sudo sh
Some Chinese ISPs block the CDN. Manually download the binary from https://d0.all7.cc/kaitu/k2r/LATEST/ and scp it to the router.
Re-run the install command. The script swaps the binary and restarts the service.

step4.title: Set up & activate
step4.subtitle: On first launch, the admin panel asks you to bind a Overleap account and paste a license key (or k2subs subscription URL).

steps.title: Open the admin panel
steps.body: Visit http://<router-IP>:9000. You'll be prompted to set an admin password the first time.

steps.title: Sign in to your Overleap account
steps.body: Use the email associated with your Overleap purchase; you'll receive a one-time code.

steps.title: Paste your k2subs subscription
steps.body: On the Smart Server tab, paste your k2subs:// URL. The router auto-picks the best server.

steps.title: Devices connect transparently
steps.body: Any device on this router's Wi-Fi or LAN now uses the VPN automatically — TVs, consoles, smart speakers, no per-device setup.

items.q: What if flashing bricks my router?
items.a: ARM routers have U-Boot recovery (short-circuit + TFTP back to stock), but full bricks happen. If you're uncertain, the pre-flashed Overleap K2 Mini / K2-001 (bottom of page) eliminates this risk.
Manufacturers usually deny warranty claims for 'unauthorized modification'. Buying second-hand or OEM units to flash is one workaround; pre-flashed Overleap units come with their own warranty.

items.q: Can routers outside this list run k2r?
items.a: Yes — any router with arm64 / armv7 / amd64 / mipsle running OpenWrt or Linux works. Performance scales with CPU: old MIPS routers do ~100Mbps; A53×4 @ 2GHz saturates gigabit.

items.q: Do I need to know the command line?
items.a: Step 3 needs one SSH command (use PuTTY or Termius — beginner-friendly). Step 4 is fully GUI in the browser.

items.q: How much RAM does k2r use?
items.a: Binary is ~17MB; runtime usage is ~30-50MB. Comfortable on 256MB RAM entry-level routers.

vsClient.title: Router vs. client app
vsClient.subtitle: Why we recommend the router approach

router.title: Smart router
items: All household devices connect automatically, no per-device setup
Always on — works the moment you power up any device
Supports smart TVs, consoles, smart speakers — anything that connects to Wi-Fi
No technical learning curve — even kids and grandparents can use it

client.title: Client app
items: Install and configure on every device
Smart TVs, consoles, smart speakers not supported
Maintain login state across every device

presale.title: Don't want to flash? Get a pre-flashed Overleap router
presale.subtitle: We also sell ready-to-use routers with k2r pre-installed. Plug in and go — 1-year warranty included.
presale.presalePriceConsultFull: Pre-order pricing — contact us · 1-year warranty · activation code included
